nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Humphrey Hill was born in 1410, his father, Sir Griffith Hill, was 95 and his mother, Viscountess Margaret De warren, was 38. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird. He died in 1484, in Court of Hill, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in Whittington, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Humphrey Hill est né en 1410, son père, Sir Griffith Hill, avait 95 ans et sa mère, Viscountess Margaret De warren, avait 38 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird. Il est décédé en 1484, à Court of Hill, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre.

Er hatte mit Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1484 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Court of Hill, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Whittington, Shropshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Humphrey Hillが1410年に生まれた時，父Sir Griffith Hillは95歳，母Viscountess Margaret De warrenは38歳であった。彼はViscountess Ann Agnes Birdとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1484年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアコウルト オフ ヒルで74歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンにて埋葬された。

ko.Court of Hill,Whitemere, Castle, Shropsh: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 쿠르트 프 힐
ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey Hill(이)가 1410년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Griffith Hill(은)는 95세였고, 그의 어머니 Viscountess Margaret De warren(은)는 38세였다. 그는 Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1484년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 쿠르트 프 힐에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Humphrey Hill родился в 1410 г., его отцу по имени Sir Griffith Hill было 95 лет, а матери, которую звали Viscountess Margaret De warren, 38. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird. Он умер в 1484 г. в местности коурт оф хилл, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия.
